orcas-island-tomorrow-forecast.content:
## 🏝️ Orcas Island Forecast – Friday, Feb 20

**High 42°F / Low 37°F** | 30% chance of rain/snow showers (after 4am Saturday)

### Ferry & Boating Conditions ⚓
**Excellent** – Winds 5 kt or less, waves under 1 ft. Small Craft Advisory lifts Thursday 10am. Calm seas all day Friday.

### Outdoor Activities
Light SE winds (~6 mph) and dry conditions make Friday ideal for hiking, kayaking, or exploring. Bundle up for chilly temps.

**Bottom line:** Perfect day for ferry crossings and outdoor adventures. Weather turns wet late Friday night.

**Friday looks great for travel** - winds will be light (5 kt or less during the day), waves minimal, and no active marine advisories. The Small Craft Advisory clears Thursday morning. Plan any ferry crossings or boat activities with confidence. Rain/snow showers arrive later Friday night after 4am, so daytime activities should be dry.

wa-road-conditions.content:
## Washington Road Conditions — Feb 19, 2026

**I-5 Seattle Corridor** ⚠️ DELAYS
- Ship Canal Bridge: 2 NB lanes closed (through 2027)
- Commute times doubled; midweek worst
- Hwy 99 at 1st Ave S Bridge: 2 NB lanes closed, 25 mph limit

**Highway 99** ⚠️ CAUTION
- Backup onto Hwy 509; no reopening ETA

**I-90 Snoqualmie Pass** ✅ CLEAR
- No closures; winter conditions possible, check for chain requirements

**Highway 20 (North Cascades)** 🚫 CLOSED
- Closed at MP 134 eastward until April/May
- Cascade River Road closed at MP 21
- Stehekin Valley Road closed

**SR-20 to Anacortes** ✅ CLEAR
- Ferry terminal accessible; ferries operating

**Ferries** ⚠️ CAUTION
- Feb 20: Bremerton 10:15pm sailing canceled
- Kingston terminal: construction delays through July

📱 Check WSDOT real-time map before travel
wa-road-conditions.rawContent:
Now I have comprehensive information. Here's the summary:

---

## Washington State Road Conditions Report
**As of February 19, 2026**

### 🚧 I-5 Seattle Corridor (MAJOR DELAYS)

**Revive I-5 Project - Ship Canal Bridge Repaving:**
- **Two northbound lanes CLOSED** on Ship Canal Bridge through 2026-2027
- Express lanes point north all day to compensate
- **Morning commute impact:** Lynnwood→Downtown now averages **50+ minutes** (was 35 min)
- **Southbound morning traffic** severely impacted - no express lane relief
- Highway 99 and I-405 also congested from diverted traffic
- **Tip:** Fridays are significantly lighter; midweek (Tues-Thurs) worst

**Highway 99 at First Avenue South Bridge (TODAY):**
- **Two northbound lanes CLOSED** for maintenance (as of 11pm Feb 18)
- Speed limit reduced to **25 mph**
- Traffic backing up onto Highway 509
- **No ETA for reopening**

---

### ⛷️ I-90 Snoqualmie Pass
- No specific closures reported in current news
- **Winter driving conditions likely** - chains may be required
- Check WSDOT real-time map before travel: https://wsdot.com/Travel/Real-time/Map/

---

### ❄️ Highway 20 (North Cascades Highway) - **PARTIALLY CLOSED**

**As of February 18, 2026:**
- **CLOSED at MP 134** (Ross Dam Trailhead) eastward
- Typical winter closure due to snow/avalanche hazards
- **Expected reopening:** April or early May (weather dependent)
- **Cascade River Road:** CLOSED at milepost 21 due to December flooding/landslides
- **Stehekin Valley Road:** CLOSED due to landslides (Dec 10) - do not travel to Stehekin area

---

### ⛴️ SR-20 to Anacortes (San Juan Islands Ferry)
- **Road is OPEN** to Anacortes ferry terminal
- Ferry service operating normally

**Ferry Alerts:**
- **Feb 20:** Bremerton→Seattle 10:15pm sailing CANCELED (refueling)
- **Starting March 1:** 3% credit/debit card fee on all fares
- **Kingston terminal:** Construction through July 2026; 20-30 min delays on 9:40pm Kingston→Edmonds (Tues-Thurs)

---

### 📱 Resources
- **WSDOT Real-Time Map:** https://wsdot.com/Travel/Real-time/Map/
- **Ferry Vessel Watch:** https://www.wsdot.com/ferries/vesselwatch/
- **Ferry Alerts Signup:** https://wsdot.wa.gov/Ferries/RouteAlerts/

travel-summary.content:
**TRAVEL ADVISORY: Seattle → Orcas Island**
**Friday, February 20, 2026**

---

**OVERALL RECOMMENDATION: GOOD TRAVEL DAY** ✅

Dry weather, calm seas, and clear roads to Anacortes make this an excellent day for the trip. Primary challenge is Seattle-area traffic congestion.

---

**KEY CONDITIONS**

- **Temperature:** 30°F morning (feels 26°F), rising to 35-42°F
- **Precipitation:** 0% until late Friday night
- **Ferry:** Excellent — calm seas, waves under 1 ft, light winds

---

**TRAFFIC ALERTS**

- **HIGH:** I-5 Ship Canal Bridge construction (2 lanes closed) — expect doubled commute times
- **MODERATE:** Hwy 99 congested — not recommended as alternate
- SR-20 to Anacortes terminal is CLEAR

---

**RECOMMENDED DEPARTURE**

Leave Seattle by **7:00-8:00 AM**. Budget 2+ hours for the 90-minute drive due to I-5 delays. Early departure ensures morning ferry and maximum island daylight.

---

**PACK LIST**

- Warm winter coat, gloves, hat, layers
- Hot beverages for ferry
- WSDOT app for traffic updates
- Full tank of gas (limited stations on Orcas)

---

**RETURN NOTE:** Weather turns wet late Friday night — plan return timing accordingly.
travel-summary.rawContent:
# 🚗⛴️ Seattle → Orcas Island Travel Advisory
**Friday, February 20, 2026**

---

## 1️⃣ Current Conditions (as of Feb 19)

| Location | Temp | Conditions |
|----------|------|------------|
| Seattle | 38°F (feels 31°F) | Overcast, 73% humidity, N winds 10 mph |

**Road Status:** I-5 has significant delays (Ship Canal Bridge construction). SR-20 to Anacortes ferry terminal is **CLEAR**.

---

## 2️⃣ Tomorrow's Travel Outlook

| | Seattle | Orcas Island |
|--|---------|--------------|
| **Morning** | 30°F (feels 26°F) | 37°F |
| **Afternoon** | 35°F | 42°F |
| **Precipitation** | 0% | 0% (until late night) |
| **Winds** | Light 2-6 mph | Light 5-6 mph |

**Ferry Conditions:** ✅ **EXCELLENT** — Calm seas, waves under 1 ft, winds 5 kt or less

---

## 3️⃣ Recommended Departure Time

**🕗 Depart Seattle by 7:00-8:00 AM**

- Morning I-5 delays double commute times (midweek worst)
- ~90 min drive to Anacortes under normal conditions; budget **2+ hours**
- Arrive early to catch a morning ferry and maximize daylight on the island

---

## 4️⃣ What to Pack/Prepare

- 🧥 **Warm winter coat** — morning temps near 30°F
- 🧤 **Gloves, hat, layers** — feels-like temps in mid-20s
- ☕ **Hot beverages** for the ferry ride
- 📱 **WSDOT app** for real-time traffic updates
- ⛽ **Full tank of gas** — limited stations on Orcas

---

## 5️⃣ Alerts & Concerns

| Priority | Issue |
|----------|-------|
| ⚠️ **HIGH** | I-5 Ship Canal Bridge: 2 lanes closed, expect doubled commute times |
| ⚠️ **MODERATE** | Hwy 99 backup — avoid as alternate route |
| ℹ️ **INFO** | Weather turns wet late Friday night — plan return accordingly |

---

### ✅ Bottom Line

**Great day to travel!** Dry weather, calm ferry conditions, and clear roads to Anacortes. The main challenge is **Seattle traffic delays** — leave early to avoid stress. Bundle up for the cold morning start.
